response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software craftsman with strengths in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and some C/C++ exposure, but the role requires 8 years of C++ programming and 5 years building large-scale infrastructure/distributed systems as a baseline. The resume shows only a brief NI LabWindows/CVI stint with C/C++ (~6 months) and no demonstrated cloud compute platform leadership or formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers. Distributed-systems exposure exists (Sociomantic micro-services) but in DLang, not C++, and there is no production cloud architecture track record matching an internal compute platform tech lead role.
strengths: Broad full-stack and systems experience across web, embedded, and high-throughput micro-services
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring via workshops and talks
Founder/owner of a long-running open-source platform deployed on Kubernetes, showing architectural ownership
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ requirement — only a short C/C++ stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015
No demonstrated experience leading or architecting a large-scale cloud compute platform in production
No formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers as required by preferred qualifications

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud compute platform / large-scale infra leadership
Strong craftsmanship, mentoring, and distributed-systems exposure
